title: sloanea brachytepala
intro: Sloanea brachytepala, also known as the 'Palo de Agua', is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, native to Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Sloanea brachytepala are attributed to its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and treat various digestive issues. Sloanea brachytepala is often used in traditional herbal medicine, with preparations including decoctions, infusions, and ointments.
